business.type_label: Portugal travel-planning advisor (independent consultant + digital-guide shop + blog)
business.geography_hint: Portugal (advisor based there since 2007); audience = English-speaking travellers planning Portugal trips

engine.ladder_reasoning: Julie sells defined expertise (paid Portugal trip-planning consultation) and the whole site leans on credentials, Google reviews, awards and testimonials to make the visitor trust the PERSON — first rung of the ladder, sells defined expertise → trust.

goal.one_action: Book a paid trip-planning consultation call (secondary: grab the free starter pack, buy a DIY guide)

structure.archetype_note: Content-marketing expert site: blog + destination guides feed a services funnel (booked consultation) alongside a WooCommerce digital-product shop and a ConvertKit newsletter — three parallel conversion paths, not one.

mapped.observed_as: Portugal travel planning, guided by experience
Discover Portugal, from the Inside / Hi, I'm Julie, a Portugal-based travel planning advisor
GET EXPERT HELP WITH YOUR PORTUGAL PLANS
Services Offered / What you get with a Portugal Trip Planning Consultation
Testimonials / What travellers value most about working with me
Industry recognition (BTL Travel Blogger Awards 2018) / Invited speaker at international travel conferences
This tends to work well if: / Why this can save you time, money and second-guessing
How does a Portugal Trip Planning Consultation work?
Cost — EUR175 for up to 60 minutes
Ready to move forward with confidence?
Stress-Free Portugal Planning Starts Here / Free. Instant download. (first name + email)
Office Hours / Working Together / Suggestions and Complaints (contact page)

unmapped.observed: Shop product grid — 12+ downloadable guides EUR9-EUR64 each with Add to basket
unmapped.why_no_fit: A priced, add-to-cart digital-product catalog is a store/checkout surface; no canonical section models a purchasable product listing (pricing = a single offer's price table, not a multi-SKU catalog).

confidence.why: fetch_quality=rendered (only contact page raw-rendered); home/about/consultation/shop captured via WebFetch and summarized by an intermediate model, so section text is paraphrase-level. Engine and multi-goal shape are clear, but section-level fidelity is soft.

gap_findings: Site's job is NOT one conversion action: three parallel goals coexist (Calendly-booked paid consultation, ConvertKit newsletter starter-pack, WooCommerce digital-product checkout) — outside the single-conversion ICP.

WooCommerce-style shop with a multi-SKU priced catalog + cart/checkout has no canonical section or engine expression; 'catalog' capability flags it but taxonomy cannot render a purchasable product listing.
Content-marketing/blog-funnel archetype (blog + destination guides driving a services page) isn't a first-class engine — trust engine captures the persuasion but not the top-of-funnel content role.
Google-reviews embed + awards logo-strip both map to socialProof, collapsing two distinct proof mechanisms (live review widget vs. award badge) into one id.
